## 2. DATABASE SCHEMA

### Table: `profiles`
Extends Supabase `auth.users`. Created automatically via trigger on user signup.

```sql
CREATE TABLE public.profiles (
  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  email         TEXT NOT NULL,
  full_name     TEXT,
  avatar_url    TEXT,
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Trigger: auto-create profile on auth.users insert
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, email, full_name, avatar_url)
  VALUES (
    NEW.id,
    NEW.email,
    NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name',
    NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'avatar_url'
  );
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

CREATE TRIGGER on_auth_user_created
  AFTER INSERT ON auth.users
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user();
```

```sql
  usage_type,
  period_start,
  SUM(quantity) AS total_usage
```

```sql
-- Trigger to update survey response_count
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_survey_response_count()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  UPDATE public.surveys
  SET response_count = (
    SELECT COUNT(*) FROM public.survey_responses
    WHERE survey_id = NEW.survey_id AND is_complete = true
  ),
  last_response_at = NEW.completed_at
  WHERE id = NEW.survey_id;
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

CREATE TRIGGER on_response_completed
  AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OF is_complete ON public.survey_responses
  FOR EACH ROW
  WHEN (NEW.is_complete = true)
  EXECUTE FUNCTION update_survey_response_count();
```

```sql
-- Helper function: check if user is member of org
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.is_org_member(org_id UUID)
RETURNS BOOLEAN AS $$
  SELECT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.organization_members
    WHERE organization_id = org_id AND user_id = auth.uid()
  );
$$ LANGUAGE sql SECURITY DEFINER STABLE;

-- Helper function: check if user is admin/owner of org
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.is_org_admin(org_id UUID)
RETURNS BOOLEAN AS $$
  SELECT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.organization_members
    WHERE organization_id = org_id
      AND user_id = auth.uid()
      AND role IN ('owner', 'admin')
  );
$$ LANGUAGE sql SECURITY DEFINER STABLE;

-- PROFILES
CREATE POLICY "Users can view own profile"
  ON public.profiles FOR SELECT
  USING (id = auth.uid());

CREATE POLICY "Users can update own profile"
  ON public.profiles FOR UPDATE
  USING (id = auth.uid());

-- Users can view profiles of people in their orgs
CREATE POLICY "Users can view org members profiles"
  ON public.profiles FOR SELECT
  USING (
    id IN (
      SELECT om2.user_id FROM public.organization_members om1
      JOIN public.organization_members om2 ON om1.organization_id = om2.organization_id
      WHERE om1.user_id = auth.uid()
    )
  );

-- ORGANIZATIONS
CREATE POLICY "Members can view their organizations"
  ON public.organizations FOR SELECT
  USING (public.is_org_member(id));

CREATE POLICY "Admins can update their organizations"
  ON public.organizations FOR UPDATE
  USING (public.is_org_admin(id));

CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can create organizations"
  ON public.organizations FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = created_by);

-- ORGANIZATION_MEMBERS
CREATE POLICY "Members can view org members"
  ON public.organization_members FOR SELECT
  USING (public.is_org_member(organization_id));

CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage org members"
  ON public.organization_members FOR ALL
  USING (public.is_org_admin(organization_id));

-- SUBSCRIPTIONS
CREATE POLICY "Members can view subscription"
  ON public.subscriptions FOR SELECT
  USING (public.is_org_member(organization_id));

-- Admin-only update (through service role in webhooks)

-- DOCUMENTS (DocAI)
CREATE POLICY "Members can view org documents"
  ON public.documents FOR SELECT
  USING (public.is_org_member(organization_id));

CREATE POLICY "Members can insert documents"
  ON public.documents FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (public.is_org_member(organization_id));

CREATE POLICY "Members can update org documents"
  ON public.documents FOR UPDATE
  USING (public.is_org_member(organization_id));

CREATE POLICY "Admins can delete documents"
  ON public.documents FOR DELETE
  USING (public.is_org_admin(organization_id) OR uploaded_by = auth.uid());

-- EXTRACTION_TEMPLATES
CREATE POLICY "Members can view templates"
  ON public.extraction_templates FOR SELECT
  USING (public.is_org_member(organization_id));

CREATE POLICY "Members can create templates"
  ON public.extraction_templates FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (public.is_org_member(organization_id));

CREATE POLICY "Admins can update templates"
  ON public.extraction_templates FOR UPDATE
  USING (public.is_org_admin(organization_id) OR created_by = auth.uid());

CREATE POLICY "Admins can delete templates"
  ON public.extraction_templates FOR DELETE
  USING (public.is_org_admin(organization_id) OR created_by = auth.uid());

-- EXTRACTION_RESULTS
CREATE POLICY "Members can view results"
  ON public.extraction_results FOR SELECT
  USING (public.is_org_member(organization_id));

-- Insert/update via service role only (from API routes)

-- SURVEYS
CREATE POLICY "Members can view surveys"
  ON public.surveys FOR SELECT
  USING (public.is_org_member(organization_id));

CREATE POLICY "Members can create surveys"
  ON public.surveys FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (public.is_org_member(organization_id));

CREATE POLICY "Members can update surveys"
  ON public.surveys FOR UPDATE
  USING (public.is_org_member(organization_id));

CREATE POLICY "Admins can delete surveys"
  ON public.surveys FOR DELETE
  USING (public.is_org_admin(organization_id) OR created_by = auth.uid());

-- SURVEY_QUESTIONS
CREATE POLICY "Members can view questions"
  ON public.survey_questions FOR SELECT
  USING (
    survey_id IN (
      SELECT id FROM public.surveys WHERE public.is_org_member(organization_id)
    )
  );

CREATE POLICY "Members can manage questions"
  ON public.survey_questions FOR ALL
  USING (
    survey_id IN (
      SELECT id FROM public.surveys WHERE public.is_org_member(organization_id)
    )
  );

-- SURVEY_RESPONSES (public access for respondents, read for org members)
CREATE POLICY "Anyone can insert responses to active surveys"
  ON public.survey_responses FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (
    survey_id IN (SELECT id FROM public.surveys WHERE status = 'active')
  );

CREATE POLICY "Members can view responses"
  ON public.survey_responses FOR SELECT
  USING (
    survey_id IN (
      SELECT id FROM public.surveys WHERE public.is_org_member(organization_id)
    )
  );

-- SURVEY_ANSWERS (public insert, member read)
CREATE POLICY "Anyone can insert answers"
  ON public.survey_answers FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (
    response_id IN (SELECT id FROM public.survey_responses)
  );

CREATE POLICY "Members can view answers"
  ON public.survey_answers FOR SELECT
  USING (
    question_id IN (
      SELECT sq.id FROM public.survey_questions sq
      JOIN public.surveys s ON sq.survey_id = s.id
      WHERE public.is_org_member(s.organization_id)
    )
  );

-- SURVEY_ANALYSES
CREATE POLICY "Members can view analyses"
  ON public.survey_analyses FOR SELECT
  USING (public.is_org_member(organization_id));
```

### Supabase Storage Buckets

```sql
-- Create storage buckets
INSERT INTO storage.buckets (id, name, public) VALUES
  ('documents', 'documents', false),       -- Private: user uploaded documents
  ('exports', 'exports', false),           -- Private: generated exports
  ('avatars', 'avatars', true);            -- Public: user/org avatars

-- Storage RLS policies
CREATE POLICY "Org members can upload documents"
  ON storage.objects FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (
    bucket_id = 'documents'
    AND (storage.foldername(name))[1] IN (
      SELECT id::text FROM public.organizations
      WHERE public.is_org_member(id)
    )
  );

CREATE POLICY "Org members can read documents"
  ON storage.objects FOR SELECT
  USING (
    bucket_id = 'documents'
    AND (storage.foldername(name))[1] IN (
      SELECT id::text FROM public.organizations
      WHERE public.is_org_member(id)
    )
  );
```

**Request/Response formats:**

```typescript
// POST /api/docai/process
// Request:
{
  documentId: string;
  templateId?: string;        // Optional: use specific template
  documentType?: DocumentType; // Optional: hint for AI
}
// Response:
{
  id: string;
  status: "processing";
  estimatedTime: number;      // Estimated seconds
}

// GET /api/docai/documents/[id]
// Response:
{
  document: {
    id: string;
    name: string;
    status: "completed";
    fileUrl: string;          // Signed URL, expires in 1 hour
    pageCount: number;
    createdAt: string;
  };
  results: {
    extractedData: Record<string, {
      value: string;
      confidence: number;
    }>;
    rawText: string;
    confidenceScore: number;
  };
}
```

## 5. STEP-BY-STEP IMPLEMENTATION ORDER

### Phase 1: Project Foundation (Days 1-3)

**Step 1.1: Project Scaffolding**
- Initialize Next.js 14+ with App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
- Install and configure shadcn/ui (run `npx shadcn-ui@latest init`)
- Install all dependencies: `@supabase/ssr`, `@supabase/supabase-js`, `stripe`, `openai`, `recharts`, `react-hook-form`, `@hookform/resolvers`, `zod`, `nanoid`, `date-fns`, `xlsx`, `lucide-react`, `@tanstack/react-query`, `@dnd-kit/core` (for drag and drop)
- Configure `tsconfig.json` with strict mode, path aliases (`@/`)
- Set up ESLint + Prettier configuration
- Create `.env.example` with all required variables

**Step 1.2: Supabase Setup**
- Create Supabase project
- Run all migration files (tables, types, functions, RLS policies, storage buckets)
- Generate TypeScript types from database (`supabase gen types typescript`)
- Set up Supabase clients: browser client (`lib/supabase/client.ts`), server client (`lib/supabase/server.ts`), admin client (`lib/supabase/admin.ts`)

**Step 1.3: Core Utilities**
- Create `lib/utils/cn.ts` (class name merger)
- Create all Zod validation schemas (`lib/validators/`)
- Create TypeScript type definitions (`types/`)
- Create standardized API response helpers (`lib/api/response.ts`, `lib/api/errors.ts`)
- Create site config (`config/site.ts`, `config/navigation.ts`)

**Step 1.4: Root Layout + Providers**
- Create root layout with fonts (Inter from Google Fonts), metadata
- Create ThemeProvider (dark/light mode via next-themes)
- Create QueryProvider (TanStack Query)
- Create ToastProvider (shadcn toast)
- Install shadcn/ui components: button, card, input, form, dialog, dropdown, table, tabs, badge, toast, skeleton, separator, sheet, avatar, command, popover, select, switch, slider, progress, alert

### Phase 2: Authentication (Days 3-5)

**Step 2.1: Supabase Auth Configuration**
- Configure Supabase Auth providers (email/password + Google OAuth)
- Set up auth redirect URLs in Supabase dashboard
- Create auth callback route handler (`app/api/auth/callback/route.ts`)

**Step 2.2: Auth Middleware**
- Create Next.js middleware (`middleware.ts`) that:
  - Refreshes Supabase session on every request
  - Redirects unauthenticated users from `/dashboard/*` to `/login`
  - Redirects authenticated users from `/login`, `/signup` to `/dashboard`
  - Checks for onboarding completion

**Step 2.3: Auth Pages**
- Build auth layout (centered card)
- Build login page with form (React Hook Form + Zod)
- Build signup page
- Build forgot-password and reset-password pages
- Build OAuth buttons component (Google)
- Create auth server actions (`actions/auth-actions.ts`)

**Step 2.4: Auth Utilities**
- Create `lib/auth/get-session.ts` -- get session in Server Components
- Create `lib/auth/get-user.ts` -- get user with profile
- Create `lib/auth/require-auth.ts` -- guard for Server Components
- Create `useUser` hook for Client Components

### Phase 3: Organization & Onboarding (Days 5-7)

**Step 3.1: Onboarding Flow**
- Build onboarding layout (step indicator)
- Build Step 1: Create Organization (form, server action)
- Build Step 2: Invite Team (email list, server action)
- Build Step 3: Choose Module (card selection)
- Auto-create subscription record (free tier) on org creation

**Step 3.2: Organization Context**
- Create OrgProvider (React context)
- Create `useOrganization` hook
- Create `lib/auth/require-org.ts` -- checks org membership in Server Components
- Store active organization in cookie/localStorage for multi-org support

**Step 3.3: Dashboard Shell**
- Build dashboard layout with sidebar and header

## 7. KEY TECHNICAL DECISIONS AND TRADE-OFFS

### Decision 1: Server Components as Default vs. Client Components Everywhere
**Choice:** Server Components by default. Client Components only when necessary (forms, charts, drag-and-drop, modals, real-time status).

**Rationale:** Server Components reduce JavaScript bundle size, enable direct database access (via Supabase server client), improve initial page load performance, and simplify data fetching. Approximately 60-70% of pages can be pure Server Components.

**Trade-off:** Requires careful architecture to pass data from server to client boundaries. Some state sharing patterns become more complex. Must use `"use client"` directive thoughtfully.

**Affected files:** Every `page.tsx` in `(dashboard)` route group starts as a Server Component. Interactive sub-components are marked `"use client"`.

### Decision 2: Supabase RLS vs. Application-Level Authorization
**Choice:** Primary authorization via Supabase RLS policies, supplemented by application-level checks for complex business logic (quota enforcement, plan gating).

**Rationale:** RLS provides defense-in-depth at the database level. Even if application code has a bug, data cannot leak across organizations. Every query automatically filters by the authenticated user's permissions.

**Trade-off:** RLS policies use SQL functions which can be harder to test and debug than TypeScript code. Complex policies can impact query performance (mitigated by proper indexing). Some operations require the service role key to bypass RLS (webhook handlers, AI processing writes).

**Affected files:** `supabase/migrations/00008_create_rls_policies.sql`, `lib/supabase/admin.ts` (service role client for bypassing RLS).

### Decision 3: Direct OpenAI Calls vs. Background Job Queue
**Choice:** Direct calls in API routes for Phase 1, with architecture prepared for background jobs.

**Rationale:** Vercel Pro plan supports function timeouts up to 300 seconds, sufficient for most document processing (GPT-4o Vision typically responds in 5-30 seconds). Adding a queue system (Inngest, Trigger.dev) adds significant complexity. The polling pattern for status updates is simpler to implement initially.

**Trade-off:** Long-running requests may time out on Vercel Hobby (10s limit) or for very large documents. No automatic retry on failure. No parallel processing of batch uploads. The `status` field on documents and polling in the UI mitigate this -- if a request times out, the status remains "processing" and the user can retry.

**Migration path:** When needed, add Inngest (`inngest/functions/process-document.ts`) and change the API route to enqueue rather than process directly. The database schema already supports this pattern with the `status` field.

**Affected files:** `src/app/api/docai/process/route.ts`, `src/lib/openai/docai-processor.ts`.

### Decision 4: File Upload Strategy -- Client Direct vs. Server Proxy
**Choice:** Client uploads directly to Supabase Storage using signed upload URLs, then notifies the API.

**Rationale:** Avoids Vercel's 4.5MB request body limit. Enables upload progress tracking on the client. Reduces server load (no proxying large files). Supabase Storage handles multipart uploads natively.

**Trade-off:** Requires exposing Supabase Storage URL to the client (mitigated by signed URLs and bucket-level RLS). File validation happens after upload rather than before. Must clean up orphaned uploads if the user abandons the flow.

**Flow:**
1. Client requests signed upload URL from API route
2. Client uploads file directly to Supabase Storage
3. Client calls `POST /api/docai/upload` with file metadata
4. Server creates document record, triggers processing

**Affected files:** `src/hooks/use-file-upload.ts`, `src/app/api/docai/upload/route.ts`, `src/components/shared/file-upload-zone.tsx`.

### Decision 5: Multi-Tenancy Model -- Organization-Based
**Choice:** Organization-based multi-tenancy where all data is scoped to an `organization_id`.

**Rationale:** Supports the team management requirement. Enables future multi-org support. Clean data isolation. Every table (except `profiles`) has an `organization_id` foreign key.

**Trade-off:** Users must create an organization even if working solo (handled in onboarding -- auto-creates a personal org). Slightly more complex queries (always filter by org_id). Must handle the case where a user belongs to multiple organizations.

**Affected files:** `src/providers/org-provider.tsx`, `src/lib/auth/require-org.ts`, every data-fetching function.

### Decision 6: State Management -- No Global Store
**Choice:** No Redux/Zustand. Use TanStack Query for server state, React Context for UI state.

**Rationale:** Server Components handle most data fetching. TanStack Query provides caching, refetching, and optimistic updates for Client Component data needs. The app has relatively little cross-cutting client state (just current org, theme, sidebar state).

**Trade-off:** More prop drilling in some cases. Must be disciplined about cache invalidation with TanStack Query. Complex mutations require careful optimistic update logic.

**Affected files:** `src/providers/query-provider.tsx`, all custom hooks in `src/hooks/`.

### Decision 7: AI Prompt Architecture -- Versioned Prompt Files
**Choice:** Store all AI prompts in dedicated TypeScript files under `src/lib/prompts/`, structured as template functions.

**Rationale:** Prompts are the core IP of the product. Versioning them in code enables Git history, code review for prompt changes, and A/B testing. Template functions accept dynamic parameters (field names, document types).

**Trade-off:** Prompt changes require code deployment (vs. a database-driven prompt management system). For Phase 1, this is acceptable; a prompt management UI can be added later for Enterprise customers.

**Example:**
```typescript
// src/lib/prompts/docai/custom-template.ts
export function buildCustomExtractionPrompt(
  fields: TemplateField[],
  documentType: string
): string {
  const fieldInstructions = fields.map(f =>
    `- "${f.name}" (${f.type}): ${f.description}${f.required ? ' [REQUIRED]' : ''}`
  ).join('\n');

  return `You are a document data extraction specialist.
Analyze this ${documentType} document image and extract the following fields:

${fieldInstructions}

Return a JSON object where each key is the field name and each value is an object with:
- "value": the extracted value (use null if not found)
- "confidence": a number from 0.0 to 1.0 indicating your confidence

Only return valid JSON. Do not include any other text.`;
}
```

### Decision 8: Survey Response Collection -- Anonymous by Default
**Choice:** Public survey endpoints require no authentication. Respondents are anonymous by default (identifiable only by a random session ID and optional metadata).

**Rationale:** Maximizing response rates is critical for survey tools. Requiring login would drastically reduce completion rates. Anonymous responses are standard in the industry.

**Trade-off:** Vulnerable to spam/abuse (mitigated by IP-based rate limiting, optional CAPTCHA, response limits per survey). Cannot link responses to user accounts. Must store respondent metadata carefully for privacy compliance.

**Affected files:** `src/app/api/public/surveys/[shareId]/respond/route.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limiter.ts`.

### Decision 9: Export Strategy -- Server-Side Generation
**Choice:** Generate export files on the server, store temporarily in Supabase Storage, return signed download URL.

**Rationale:** Large exports can exceed browser memory. Server-side generation handles any size. Signed URLs expire after download, improving security. The `xlsx` library works well in Node.js.

**Trade-off:** Export generation takes server resources. Large exports may need background processing. Must clean up temporary files.

**Affected files:** `src/lib/export/csv-exporter.ts`, `src/lib/export/excel-exporter.ts`, `src/app/api/docai/export/route.ts`.

### Decision 10: Deployment Architecture
**Choice:** Single Vercel deployment with Edge middleware, Serverless functions for API routes, and ISR for marketing pages.

**Rationale:** Vercel provides zero-config deployment for Next.js, automatic HTTPS, CDN, preview deployments for PRs, and environment variable management. The entire stack (Next.js + Supabase + Stripe + OpenAI) is designed for serverless.

**Trade-off:** Serverless cold starts (mitigated by Vercel's edge network). Function timeout limits (mitigated by Vercel Pro plan's 300s limit). No persistent WebSocket connections (use polling or Supabase Realtime instead).

**Environment variables required:**

```
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=

# Stripe
```

## Middleware Architecture (`middleware.ts`)

The Next.js middleware is a critical file that handles authentication, session refresh, and routing logic on every request.

```typescript
// middleware.ts - Pseudocode structure
import { createServerClient } from '@supabase/ssr'
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'
import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server'

export async function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
  // 1. Create Supabase client with cookie handling
  // 2. Refresh session (call getUser to refresh token)
  // 3. Define route protection rules:
  //    - /dashboard/* -> require auth, redirect to /login if not
  //    - /login, /signup -> redirect to /dashboard if already authed
  //    - /onboarding -> require auth, redirect if already onboarded
  //    - /s/* -> public, no auth needed
  //    - /api/public/* -> public, no auth needed
  //    - /api/v1/* -> API key auth (handled in route, not middleware)
  //    - /api/* -> require auth (session)
  // 4. Return response with updated cookies
}

export const config = {
  matcher: [
    '/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|.*\\.(?:svg|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|webp)$).*)',
  ],
}
```

### Critical Files for Implementation

1. **`src/middleware.ts`** -- The auth gateway that protects all routes, refreshes sessions, and handles routing logic. Must be implemented first after scaffolding, as every other feature depends on it.

2. **`supabase/migrations/00008_create_rls_policies.sql`** -- Contains all Row Level Security policies that enforce data isolation across organizations. Critical for security; a single misconfigured policy can leak data between tenants.

3. **`src/lib/openai/docai-processor.ts`** -- The core AI processing logic for DocAI. Orchestrates the GPT-4o Vision API call, prompt construction from templates, response parsing, confidence scoring, and result storage. The most technically complex single file.

4. **`src/lib/stripe/webhook-handlers.ts`** -- Handles all Stripe webhook events to synchronize subscription state. Must be bulletproof (idempotent, handle out-of-order events) because billing errors directly impact revenue and user access.

5. **`src/lib/billing/plans.ts`** -- The source of truth for plan limits and feature flags. Referenced by quota checks, UI plan gates, and API middleware. Changes here cascade across the entire application's behavior.
